# Prerelease Gate — 2026-07-17 (second pass)

## Summary
- Repo: danieljustus/symaira-meet
- HEAD: c261d1abf17d5a8de8b3e95850556501a85f91b9
- Last release: none (first release)
- Proposed target: v0.4.0-beta.1 (bump: none)
- Gate: blocked

## Gate checks
| Check | Result | Detail |
|---|---|---|
| CI / tests (HEAD) | PASS | CI run 29604658290 success on push to main; CodeQL success. Local tests delegated to CI per documented CLT/XCTest gate (#58). |
| Version consistency | PASS | 12 version mentions checked across README/docs/Sources/scripts, 0 corrections needed (`0.1.0-dev` fallback and `0.1.0-test` harness are intentional). |
| README / doc currency | PASS | Prior staleness findings resolved (#61 README rewrite, #57 privacy.md); no stale capability claims found. |
| Coverage vs threshold | WARN | 37.3% lines (10245/27430) from the CI `coverage-report` artifact on HEAD (run 29604658290) — the canonical source for CLT-only machines; no hard repo gate, warn-only. |
| Coverage regression | PASS | baseline n/a (first release) — cannot regress. |
| Patch coverage | PASS | n/a (no prior tag to diff against). |
| Assets preview | PASS | All expected assets have producers in `release.yml` + `scripts/` (CLI tar.gz, signed/notarized DMG, checksums, SBOM, notices) — contingent on signing secrets, see BLOCK. |
| Distribution publishers | BLOCK | Homebrew formula writer removed by #62; formula has placeholder sha256 and an asset-name mismatch; Apple signing secrets absent (0 repo secrets, no environments). |
| Security | WARN | 0 open Dependabot alerts; 1 medium code-scanning alert (#2: ci.yml missing explicit permissions, introduced by #63) → /05-gh-security-fix. |

## Findings
- [ ] **[BLOCK][Distribution] Homebrew formula has no release writer, placeholder sha256, and an asset-name mismatch**
  - **Status quo:** `docs/getting-started.md:6-7` documents `brew install danieljustus/tap/symmeet`. `danieljustus/tap` contains `Formula/symmeet.rb` pointing to `.../symmeet_0.4.0-beta.1_darwin_arm64.tar.gz` with `sha256 "0000...0000"`. The release-workflow Homebrew publisher added in #60 was removed by #62's pipeline rework; the tap repo has no self-update workflow; no `HOMEBREW_TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN` secret exists. Separately, `scripts/package-cli.sh:12` produces `symmeet_v0.4.0-beta.1_darwin_arm64.tar.gz` (with `v` prefix), so the formula URL as written would 404 after release.
  - **Likely cause:** #62 rework dropped the publisher step; the formula was staged with placeholder values expecting that publisher to fill them.
  - **Proposed solution:** Restore a Homebrew publisher step in `release.yml` (clone `danieljustus/tap`, update the formula's `url` + `sha256` from `dist/checksums.txt`, push) and configure the tap token; fix the formula's asset name to include the `v` prefix. A documented manual tap update in `docs/releasing.md` is an acceptable beta alternative if automation is deferred deliberately.
  - **Handoff:** `/02-gh-plan --from-review` → `/03-gh-go`

- [ ] **[BLOCK][Distribution] Apple signing secrets are not configured for this repository**
  - **Status quo:** `gh secret list` shows zero repository secrets and no environments exist. `release.yml` ("Guard — required secrets present") requires `APPLE_CERTIFICATE_BASE64`, `APPLE_CERTIFICATE_PASSWORD`, `APPLE_ID`, `APPLE_TEAM_ID`, `APPLE_APP_PASSWORD` and fails fast when unset, so a tag-triggered signed release cannot run.
  - **Likely cause:** Secrets were never configured after the pipeline rework (#62).
  - **Proposed solution:** Add the five `APPLE_*` repository secrets (values live outside the repo; this is a maintainer action).
  - **Handoff:** Maintainer configures secrets, then re-run `/06-gh-prerelease`.

- [ ] **[WARN][Security] ci.yml has no explicit workflow permissions (CodeQL alert #2, medium)**
  - **Status quo:** `actions/missing-workflow-permissions` on `.github/workflows/ci.yml:10`, opened 2026-07-17T17:54Z after the coverage change in #63.
  - **Likely cause:** The CI workflow never declared a `permissions:` block; the new artifact-upload step made the default permissive token scope visible to scanning.
  - **Proposed solution:** Add `permissions: contents: read` at workflow level (artifact upload does not require broader token scope).
  - **Handoff:** `/05-gh-security-fix`

- [ ] **[WARN][Coverage] Absolute line coverage is 37.3%**
  - **Status quo:** 10245/27430 lines on HEAD, measured from the CI `coverage-report` artifact (the canonical coverage source established for machines without full Xcode).
  - **Likely cause:** First measured baseline; large surfaces (capture, WhisperKit, agent) are exercised by smoke/contract tests rather than unit tests.
  - **Proposed solution:** Treat 37.3% as the baseline for regression tracking on subsequent releases; grow coverage via planned coverage issues, not release-blocking test churn.
  - **Handoff:** `/test-coverage` → `/02-gh-plan --from-coverage` (post-release)

## Applied mechanical fixes (default mode)
— none — (version consistency already clean; 0 corrections needed)

## Next step
- gate=blocked → resolve the BLOCK findings via their handoff commands, then re-run `/06-gh-prerelease`
